---
title: "Mathematical Notes"
description: "Expository notes and working calculations in geometric analysis."
page-layout: full
body-classes: notes-page
toc: false
format:
  html:
    include-after-body: sidebar.html
listing:
  contents:
    - "*.qmd"
    - "*.md"
    - "!tex/README.md"
  sort: "date desc"
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 2
  fields: [title, description, date, categories]
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: true
  page-size: 100
---

::: {.notes-intro}
::: {.notes-intro-copy}
<p class="notes-kicker">GEOMETRIC ANALYSIS · EXPOSITORY NOTES</p>

<p class="notes-intro-title">$e^{i\pi}+1=0$</p>

Short notes on geometric PDE, minimal surfaces, variational methods, and the calculations behind them.
:::
:::

::: {.notes-publish}
::: {.notes-publish-copy}
### Add a note

Upload a prepared Markdown file to GitHub. Only accounts with write access to this repository can publish. After you confirm the commit, GitHub Actions validates the note, rebuilds the Quarto site, and publishes it automatically.
:::

::: {.notes-publish-actions}
[Upload Markdown on GitHub](https://github.com/XingjianZhouMath/XingjianZhouMath.github.io/upload/main/notes){.btn .btn-primary}

[Download note template](../files/note-template.md){.btn .btn-outline-primary download="note-template.md"}
:::

1. Download the template and replace its sample metadata and content.
2. Name the file with lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, such as `inverse-mean-curvature-flow.md`.
3. Upload it to GitHub and confirm the commit. A successful workflow run publishes it here after a few minutes.
:::

## Browse Notes

Expository notes and working calculations. Use the search box or topic labels to browse the collection.
